Post-hoc pairwise found that that the pooled effect of the Brief COPE was significantly more positive ($r$ = `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[2,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[2,10],3)`, `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[2,11],3)`), the CISS ($r$ = `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[4,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[4,10],3)`, `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[4,11],3)`) and the TOPS ($r$ = `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[5,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[5,10],3)`, `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[5,11],3)`) in comparison to the ACSI ($r$ = `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[1,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[1,10],3)`, `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[1,11],3)`). No significant differences were present between the ACSI and the Heterz.. & Chroni (????) hybrid measure ($r$ = `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[3,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[3,10],3)`, `r round(pair_coping_measure_stats[3,11],3)`). Cumulatively, the moderator and post-hoc analysis of the measures suggests studies using the ACSI are more likely to report a negative effect between adaptive coping strategies and anxiety in elite athletes.

This suggests studies using the Brief coping scale are more likely to demonstrate the use of coping strategies as an anxiety risk factor, regardless of the adaptive/maladaptive labelling of its respective strategies. The remaining measures had no significant pooled effect on the association between coping and anxiety, and their full details can be found in table ... .

Measurement internal consistency reliability evaluated by Cronbach's Alpha was investigated as a moderator. Measurement reliability had no significant effect on the association between Adaptive coping and anxiety (k = `r alpha_data$k`, n = `r alpha_data$n`, $QM$ = `r round(alpha_data$QM,2)`, $p$ \< .`r round(alpha_data$QM_p,3)`)01. The pooled moderating effect size of measurement reliability had a small and non significant effect ($r$ = `r round(alpha_stats$Pearson_R[2],2)`, 95% CI\[`r round(alpha_stats[2,10],3)`, `r round(alpha_stats[2,11],3)`\]).

Similarly to the percentage of female participants, there was a high proportion of missing values for measurement reliability data. In the Adaptive strategies, 50 effects within 4 studies did not report Cronbach's Alpha for their sample. Due to the high proportion of missing values, a sensitivity analysis of the effect of missing Cronbach's Alpha was conducted. The presence of missing reliability data was a significant moderator on the association between adaptive coping strategies and anxiety (k = `r alpha_NA_data$k`, n = `r alpha_NA_data$n`, $QM$ = `r round(alpha_NA_data$QM,2)`, $p$ \< .`r round(alpha_NA_data$QM_p,3)`01). For the studies that reported reliability data for their sample, the pooled effect size was not significant ($r$ = `r round(alpha_NA_stats[1,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(alpha_NA_stats[1,10],3)`, `r round(alpha_NA_stats[1,11],3)`\]), whilst for the studies that did not report sample reliabiltiy data, the pooled effect size was small and significant ($r$ = `r round(alpha_NA_stats[2,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(alpha_NA_stats[2,10],3)`, `r round(alpha_NA_stats[2,11],3)`\]). This suggests studies that did not report the internal reliability statistics of their sample tended to report stronger protective associations between adaptive coping strategies and anxiety levels in elite athletes in comparison to studies that did report sample specific reliability data.

```{r}
grand_moderator_table <- rbind(`Percentage Female`, `Gender data`, 
                               `Mean Age` ,`Coping Classification`, `Coping Measure`, `Alpha data`, `Alpha NA data`)

grand_moderator_table %>% mutate(label_ID = row_number(),
                                 label = case_when(
                                   label_ID == "1"  ~ "Percentage Female",
                                   label_ID == "2"  ~ "Presence of Gender data", 
                                   label_ID == "3"  ~ "Mean Age",
                                   label_ID == "4"  ~ "Coping Classification",
                                   label_ID == "5"  ~ "Coping Measure",
                                   label_ID == "6"  ~ "Coping Measure Reliability" ,
                                   label_ID == "7"  ~ "Presence of Coping Measure Reliability data" 
                                 )
                                 ) %>%
  select(label, n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`) %>%
  gt() %>% fmt_number(decimals = 4) %>% 
  tab_header(
    title = "Moderator fit Analysis"
  )

count_data_coping_classification <- Adaptive_data %>%
  group_by(Coping_classification) %>%
  summarise(
    k = n(),
    n = sum(n)
            ) %>% as.data.frame() 

coping_classification_stats %>% as.data.frame() %>% select(-type) %>% 
  arrange(desc(estimate)) %>%
  mutate(term = str_remove(term,"Coping_classification"),
     #    n = count_data_coping_classification$n
         ) %>%
  left_join(
    y = count_data_coping_classification,
    by = join_by(term == Coping_classification),
  ) %>%
  select(term, k, n, estimate, Pearson_R, Pearson_low, Pearson_high, p.value) %>%
  gt() %>% fmt_number(decimals = 4)%>% 
  tab_header(
    title = "Coping Classification Moderator Analysis"
  )

count_data_coping_measure <- Adaptive_data %>%
  group_by(coping_Measure_tidy) %>%
  summarise(
    k = n(),
    n = sum(n)
            ) %>% as.data.frame() 

coping_measure_stats %>% select(-type) %>% arrange(desc(estimate)) %>%
  mutate(term = str_remove(term,"coping_Measure_tidy")) %>%
  left_join(
    y = count_data_coping_measure,
    by = join_by(term == coping_Measure_tidy),
  ) %>%
  select(term, k, n, estimate, Pearson_R, Pearson_low, Pearson_high, p.value) %>%
  gt() %>% fmt_number(decimals = 4) %>% 
  tab_header(
    title = "Coping Measures Moderator Analysis"
  )
```

### 3.4.2 Maladaptive

```{r}
Maladaptive_data <- anxiety_data %>% filter(
  Adp_mal_classification== "Maladaptive",
 # id != 150, # bois paper outlier - removing takes estimate to .1339, p =.1149
 #  Var_2_type != "composite score" # sensitivity analysis - Q reduces from 57.02 to 40.76, and I2 goes from 18.2% to 16.2%, amd estimate magnitude increases from -.07 to -.123 (but still not sig)
     ) %>% bind_shadow()
#sum(Adaptive_data$`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`)
# Discuss fact that Da Silva paper Freedom from Worry needed rescoring (measure requires it, but paper correlation patterns suggest authors forgot to do it)
# outlier analysis needed - effect of composite scores seems to be severe, and bois measure looks to be influential
#SE_z (1 / sqrt(n-3)) or variance_z (1 /(n-3))
m.gen <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 


summary(m.gen)
#baujat(m.gen, xlim=c(0,20), ylim=c(0,0.2), bty="l", las=1)

## Model check

m_within_null <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  data = Maladaptive_data, random = list(~ 1 | effect_id, ~ 1 | Author_Year)
  , sigma2 = c(0, NA))

m_between_null <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  data = Maladaptive_data, 
  random = list(~ 1 | effect_id, ~ 1 | Author_Year), 
  sigma2 = c(NA, 0))

m_both_null <- rma.mv( 
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = list(~ 1 | effect_id, ~ 1 | Author_Year), sigma2 = c(0, 0)) 

aov_within <- anova(m.gen, m_within_null) 
aov_between <- anova(m.gen, m_between_null) 
aov_bothnull <- anova(m.gen, m_both_null) 

# Join these results in a table
aov_table <- rbind(
c(df=aov_between$p.f, aov_between$fit.stats.f[c(3:4, 1)], LRT = NA, p = NA),
c(df=aov_within$p.r, aov_within$fit.stats.r[c(3:4, 1)], LRT = aov_within$LRT, p = aov_within$pval),
c(df=aov_between$p.r, aov_between$fit.stats.r[c(3:4, 1)], LRT = aov_between$LRT, p = aov_between$pval),
c(df=aov_bothnull$p.r, aov_bothnull$fit.stats.r[c(3:4, 1)], LRT = aov_bothnull$LRT, p = aov_bothnull$pval)
)
rownames(aov_table) <- c("Three-level model", "Within-studies variance constrained", "Between-studies variance constrained", "Both variance components constrained")
as.tibble(aov_table) %>%
  mutate(
    model_label = recode(
      as.character(row.names(.)),
      `1` = "Three-level model",
      `2` = "Within-studies variance constrained",
      `3` = "Between-studies variance constrained",
      `4` = "Both variance components constrained"
    )
  ) %>% gt(rowname_col = "model_label"
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    AIC = "AIC",
    BIC = "BIC",
    ll  = "Log-likelihood",
    LRT = "LRT",
    p   = "p-value"
  )
```

```{r, echo = FALSE}

# Write up stats 
conf <- predict(m.gen)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
M_perf <- model_performance(m.gen)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

stats <- broom::tidy(m.gen, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )
model_stats <- broom::glance(m.gen)

i2 <- var.comp(m.gen)

summary(i2)
results_i2 <- i2$results
total_i2 <- i2$totalI2
#~#plot(i2)
conf_int(m.gen,
         vcov = "CR2")
coef_test(m.gen,
         vcov = "CR2")
```

A multilevel meta-analysis was conducted to examix the association between maladaptive strategies and anxiety severity. Data were drawn from `r Maladaptive_data %>% group_by(Study, level_of_sport) %>% summarise() %>% nrow()` samples (`r nrow(Maladaptive_data)` effect sizes, `r Maladaptive_data %>% group_by(Study) %>% summarise() %>% nrow()` individual studies) and a total of `r sum(Maladaptive_data$n)` participants. The pooled effect size indicated a moderate, positive and statistically significant association between maladaptive coping and anxiety *r* = `r round(stats$Pearson_R,2)`, (95% CI \[`r round(conf$ci.lb,2)`, `r round(conf$ci.ub,2)`\]), $p$ = `r round(stats$p.value, 3)`, $k$ = `r model_stats$nobs`, $n$ = `r sum(Maladaptive_data$n)`. This moderate pooled effect for the association between maladaptive coping and anxiety severity rejected the null hypothesis. . $I^2$ was `r round( total_i2, 2)` with an estimated variance components $tau^2$level 3 = `r results_i2[3,2]`, $tau^2$level 2 = `r results_i2[2,2]`, meaning that $I^2$Level 3 = `r round(results_i2[3,1],2)`% of the total variation could be attributed to between cluster, and $I^2$Level 2 = `r round(results_i2[2,1],2)`% to within cluster heterogeneity. Significant heterogeneity was detected by the Q statistic ($Q$ = `r round(M_perf$CochransQ,3)`, $p$ \< .`r round(M_perf$p_CochransQ,3)`01).

#### Plots

```{r, plots ,warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
#pdf(file='forestplot_test.pdf') 
# meta::forest(m.gen, 
#            #  sortvar = TE,
#              addpred=TRUE,
#              addcred = TRUE,
#              showweights=TRUE, 
#              header="ID", 
#              prediction = TRUE, 
#              print.tau2 = TRUE,
#          #     alim=c(-1,6),
#          # #    xlim=c(-8,7),
#          #     ylim =c(118,0), 
#              fontsize = .5,
#            # slab = paste(dat$Author_Year, ", n=", dat$n), # study labels
#              ilab = Maladaptive_data$n,
#              leftlabs = c("Author_Year", "z", "SE"),
#              layout = "RevMan5")
#addpoly(m.gen, row = -1, cex = 0.8)
#dev.off()


# dat <- escalc(measure="ZCOR", yi=mod_z, vi=variance_z, 
#               data=Maladaptive_data, slab=paste(Author_Year, effect_id))
# dat
# V <- vcalc(variance_z, cluster=Author_Year, obs=effect_id, data=Maladaptive_data, rho=0.8)
# res <- rma(yi,vi,data=dat)
# res
# agg <- aggregate(dat, cluster=Author_Year, V=vcov(res, type="obs"), addk=TRUE)
# agg
# res <- rma(yi, vi, method="REML", data=agg, digits=3, slab=Author_Year)
# res



#forest plot
# forest(res, mlab="Pooled Estimate", addpred=TRUE, header="Sample", 
#      #  studylab="sample",
#        ilab=ki, ilab.xpos=-2.7, cex=.89, order="obs", 
#        xlim=c(-8,6), at=seq(-0.5,1.5,by=0.5), showweights=TRUE)
# text(-2.7, res$k+2, "Number of Estimates", cex=.89, font=1.5)  
# text(2.7, res$k+2, "Weight", cex=.89, font=1.5)  

funnel(m.gen,
       level = c(90, 95, 99),
       shade=c("white", "gray55", "gray75"), 
       xlab= "Fischer's z",
       label=FALSE,
      # digits=c(0, 1),
       refline = stats$estimate, 
       #main="Contour-Enhanced Funnel Plot", 
      # legend = ("topleft")
       )
legend("topleft",
       legend = c(".00 < p ≤ .01" ,".01 < p ≤ .05", ".05 < p ≤ .10", ".10 < p ≤ 1.00"),
       fill   = c( "#CCCCCC", "gray75", "gray55", "white"),
       bty    = "y")

```

#### Detection of Influential Cases and Outliers

```{r}
outlier_detection <- Maladaptive_data %>% mutate(
  Upper_z_ci = (z + 1.96 * sqrt(variance_z)),
  Lower_z_ci = (z - 1.96 * sqrt(variance_z)),
  Outlier = as.factor(case_when(
    Upper_z_ci < conf$ci.lb | Lower_z_ci > conf$ci.ub ~ "outlier",
    Upper_z_ci > conf$ci.lb | Lower_z_ci < conf$ci.ub ~ "non outlier",
  )
)) 
summary(outlier_detection$Outlier)

table <- outlier_detection %>% 
  filter(Outlier == "outlier") %>% 
  select(
    Author_Year, Study_sample_type, Study_Quality,
    Var_1_summarised,`Var 2`, r, z,# variance_z,
   # Lower_z_ci,Upper_z_ci
    ) %>% 
  mutate( Pooled_effect = conf$pred, Pooled_lb_ci = conf$ci.lb, Pooled_ub_ci = conf$ci.ub) %>% #group_by(Author_Year) %>%
  arrange(desc(z), by_group = TRUE)%>% 
  rename(
  "Study" = "Author_Year" ,
  "Sample" = "Study_sample_type",
  "Study Quality" = "Study_Quality",
  "Anxiety evaluated" = "Var_1_summarised",
  "Coping Strategy" = "Var 2",
  "Study effect (Pearson's r)" =  "r",
  "Study effect (Fischer's z)" = "z",
  "Pooled effect (z)" = "Pooled_effect",
  "Pooled effect (95% CI lower bound)" = "Pooled_lb_ci",
  "Pooled effect (95% CI upper bound)" = "Pooled_ub_ci"
  ) 
  #ungroup() %>%
 

table %>%  gt() %>% fmt_number(decimals = 3) %>%
  tab_stubhead(label = "Study") %>%
tab_options(row_group.as_column = T) 

#influence(m.gen)

ggplot(outlier_detection,
       aes(x = z, colour = Outlier, fill = Outlier)) +
  # Add a histogram with transparent bars (alpha = .2)
  geom_histogram(alpha = .2) +
  # Add a vertical line at the pooled effect value (m_re$b[1])
  geom_vline(xintercept = stats$estimate) +
  labs( x= "Fischer's Z") +
  # Apply a black and white theme
  theme_bw() #+ facet_wrap(~Risk_of_Bias)

```

```{r ,message=FALSE , warning = FALSE}
sensitivity_data <- outlier_detection %>%
  filter(Outlier != "outlier")

test <- outlier_detection %>% 
  filter(z < .8)

sens_m.gen <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  data = sensitivity_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 

sens_m.gen


# meta::forest(sens_m.gen, 
#            #  sortvar = TE,
#              addpred=TRUE,
#              addcred = TRUE,
#              showweights=FALSE, 
#              header="ID", 
#              prediction = TRUE, 
#              print.tau2 = TRUE,
#          #     alim=c(-1,6),
#          # #    xlim=c(-8,7),
#          #     ylim =c(118,0), 
#              fontsize = .5,
#            # slab = paste(dat$Author_Year, ", n=", dat$n), # study labels
#              ilab = sensitivity_data$n,
#              leftlabs = c("Author_Year", "z", "SE"),
#              layout = "RevMan5")


## Write up stats

#dmetar::variance_decomposition(sens_m.gen)
conf <- predict(sens_m.gen)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
M_perf <- model_performance(sens_m.gen)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(sens_m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

sens_stats <- broom::tidy(sens_m.gen, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )
sens_model_stats <- broom::glance(sens_m.gen)

i2 <- var.comp(sens_m.gen)
summary(i2)
results_i2 <- i2$results
total_i2 <- i2$totalI2
#plot(i2)
# conf_int(m.gen,
#          vcov = "CR2")
# coef_test(m.gen,
#          vcov = "CR2")
table
```

Outlier and diagnostics were performed as significant heterogeneity was present. The Fischer's Z of `r nrow(Maladaptive_data) - nrow(sensitivity_data)` studies were outside the 95% CI range of the pooled effect. Details of these studies are presented in table ... . The association between Limited access to Emotion Regulation Strategies and Anxiety ($r$ = .75) by Hut et al.,(2023) recorded the most influential positive association, whilst the association between *Lack of Emotional Clarity* and *Somatic Anxiety* ($r$ = -.15) by Minkler et al., (2021) recorded the most influential negative association. Visual inspection in figure ... shows the identified outliers are distributed evenly, suggesting no clear indication of bias.

A sensitivity analysis was conducted with the removal of outlying effects to determine if they were influential. This revealed a similar overall effect size that was also not significant (*r* = `r round(sens_stats$Pearson_R,2)`, (95% CI \[`r round(sens_stats$Pearson_low,2)`, `r round(sens_stats$Pearson_high,2)`\]), $p$ = `r round(sens_stats$p.value, 3)`), which was comparable to the original analysis (*r* = `r round(stats$Pearson_R,2)`, (95% CI \[`r round(stats$Pearson_low,2)`, `r round(stats$Pearson_high,2)`\]), $p$ = `r round(stats$p.value, 3)`). As the sensitivity analysis suggests that removing the outliers had almost no effect on the whole whole analysis, it suggests the outliers had negligible bias on the pooled effect. The original analysis was retained for the remainder of the analyses.

#### Publication Bias

```{r}
funnel(m.gen,
       level = c(90, 95, 99),
       shade=c("white", "gray55", "gray75"), 
       xlab= "Fischer's z",
       label=FALSE,
      # digits=c(0, 1),
       refline = stats$estimate, 
       main="Contour-Enhanced Funnel Plot", 
      # legend = ("topleft")
       )
legend("topleft",
       legend = c(".00 < p ≤ .01" ,".01 < p ≤ .05", ".05 < p ≤ .10", ".10 < p ≤ 1.00"),
       fill   = c( "#CCCCCC", "gray75", "gray55", "white"),
       bty    = "y")


# Method for Egger from here : https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-sig-meta-analysis/2020-December/002471.html#:~:text=Say%20that%20data%20includes%20a%20variable%20called%20sei,paper%2C%20and%20example%20code%20in%20our%20supplementary%20materials.  and discussed in this paper https://osf.io/vqp8u_v1
## SE_z (1 / sqrt(n-3)) or variance_z (1 /(n-3))
# set model with SE_z set as moderator and variance for V.
egger_test_model <- m.gen <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~ SE_z, #
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
 # test = "t",
 # method = "REML"
) 

# Then apply cluster-robust standard errors for the RVE-based test:
eggers_results <- coef_test(egger_test_model, vcov = "CR2")  
  
# regtest(m.gen)
# trimfill(egger_test_model)
```

For adaptive coping strategies, indications of publication bias could be found. Visual inspection of the funnel plot in figure ... suggested a symmetrical distribution. Egger's regression test was adapted to the multilevel context using robust variance estimation (RVE) (Rodgers & Pustejovsky, 2021). Results of the adapted Egger's regression test was not significant, suggesting asymmetry was not influential ($z$ = `r round(eggers_results[1,2],2)`, $SE$ = `r round(eggers_results[1,3],2)` , $p$ = `r round(eggers_results[1,7],2)`).

#### Moderator Analyses

```{r MAL_female_perc_mod}
Maladaptive_data <- anxiety_data %>% filter(
  Adp_mal_classification== "Maladaptive",
 # id != 150, # bois paper outlier - removing takes estimate to .1339, p =.1149
 #  Var_2_type != "composite score" # sensitivity analysis - Q reduces from 57.02 to 40.76, and I2 goes from 18.2% to 16.2%, amd estimate magnitude increases from -.07 to -.123 (but still not sig)
  ) %>%
  mutate(percentage_female_imp = replace_na(percentage_female, 0),
         Risk_of_Bias2 = as.factor(case_when(
           Risk_of_Bias == "Low Risk" ~ "Low Risk",
           Risk_of_Bias == "Medium Risk" ~ "High Risk",
           Risk_of_Bias == "High Risk" ~ "High Risk",
         )),
     #    gender_missing = bind_shadow(percentage_female),
     ,
          `Var 2 Alpha` = as.numeric(`Var 2 Alpha`),
        coping_Measure = `emotion regulation_or_coping measure used`
         ) %>% bind_shadow()


# Gender moderator

gender_raw <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~ percentage_female,
#  mods = ~  `Var 2 Alpha`,
#  dfs="contain",
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + Coping_classification  ,
# mods = ~ -1 + Var_1_summarised  ,
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + `Mean Age`,
#  mods = ~  + Coping_classification  * Risk_of_Bias ,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 

gender_raw_conf <- predict(gender_raw)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
gender_raw_M_perf <- model_performance(gender_raw)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

gender_raw_stats <- broom::tidy(gender_raw, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )
gender_raw_model_stats <- broom::glance(gender_raw)


# generating table stats
k <- gender_raw_model_stats$nobs
n <- Maladaptive_data %>% select(z, variance_z, percentage_female, `Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit() 
n <- sum(n$`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`)
studies_effects <- Maladaptive_data %>% select( Study,
  z, variance_z, percentage_female, `Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit()%>% group_by(Study) %>% summarise(n_effects = n())
n_studies <- nrow(studies_effects)
QM <- gender_raw_model_stats$cochran.qm
`QM p` <- gender_raw_model_stats$p.value.cochran.qm
b1 <- gender_raw_stats[2,] %>% select(Pearson_R)
z <- gender_raw_stats[2,] %>% select(estimate)
`estimate p` <- gender_raw_stats[2,] %>% select(p.value)
 Percentage_Female <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`) %>% mutate(QM_p = `QM p`,
                                                                              estimate_p= `estimate p`)
`Percentage Female` <- cbind(n_studies,k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`)
```

```{r MAL_gender_present_mod}
# gender_missing <- rma.mv(
#   yi = z,
#   V = variance_z,
#   mods = ~ -1+ percentage_female_NA,
# #  dfs="contain",
# #  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + Coping_classification  ,
# # mods = ~ -1 + Var_1_summarised  ,
# #  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + `Mean Age`,
# #  mods = ~  + Coping_classification  * Risk_of_Bias ,
#   data = Maladaptive_data,
#   random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
#   test = "t",
#   method = "REML"
# ) 
# 
# gender_missing_conf <- predict(gender_missing)
# # conf$ci.lb
# # conf$ci.ub
# gender_missing_M_perf <- model_performance(gender_missing)
# # Compute robust CHE results for rho
# # CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# # CHEresultrobust
# 
# gender_missing_stats <- broom::tidy(gender_missing, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
#   mutate(
#          Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
#          Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
#          Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
#          )
# gender_missing_model_stats <- broom::glance(gender_missing)
# 
# 
# # generating table stats
# k <- gender_missing_model_stats$nobs
# n <- Adaptive_data %>% select(z, variance_z, percentage_female_NA,`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit() 
# n <- sum(n$`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`)
# 
# studies_effects <- Adaptive_data %>% select( Study,
#   z, variance_z, percentage_female_NA, `Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit()%>% group_by(Study) %>% summarise(n_effects = n())
# n_studies <- nrow(studies_effects)
# 
# QM <- gender_missing_model_stats$cochran.qm
# `QM p` <- gender_missing_model_stats$p.value.cochran.qm
# b1 <- gender_missing_stats[2,] %>% select(Pearson_R)
# z <- gender_missing_stats[2,] %>% select(estimate)
# `estimate p` <- gender_missing_stats[2,] %>% select(p.value)
#  Gender_data <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`) %>% mutate(QM_p = `QM p`,
#                                                                               estimate_p= `estimate p`)
# `Gender data` <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`)


```

```{r MAL_coping_measure_mod}

Maladaptive_data <- Maladaptive_data %>%
  mutate(coping_Measure_tidy_athlete_focus = as.factor(case_when(
    coping_Measure_tidy == "Brief COPE" ~ "Brief COPE",
    coping_Measure_tidy == "Difficulties in Emotion Regulation Scale-Short Form" ~ "Difficulties in Emotion Regulation Scale-Short Form",
    coping_Measure_tidy == "Inventory for Stressful Situations (CISS)" ~ "Inventory for Stressful Situations (CISS)",
    coping_Measure_tidy == "COPE, MCOPE, Test of performance strategies (TOPS)" ~ "Athlete specific measure",
    coping_Measure_tidy == "Test of Performance Strategies" ~ "Athlete specific measure"
  )),
    coping_Measure_tidy_athlete_focus = relevel(coping_Measure_tidy_athlete_focus,
                                                ref = "Athlete specific measure")
  )

  coping_measure <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~ -1 + coping_Measure_tidy_athlete_focus,
#  dfs="contain",
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + Coping_classification  ,
# mods = ~ -1 + Var_1_summarised  ,
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + `Mean Age`,
#  mods = ~  + Coping_classification  * Risk_of_Bias ,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 

coping_measure_conf <- predict(coping_measure)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
coping_measure_M_perf <- model_performance(coping_measure)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

coping_measure_stats <- broom::tidy(coping_measure, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )
coping_measure_model_stats <- broom::glance(coping_measure)


# generating table stats
k <- coping_measure_model_stats$nobs
n <- Maladaptive_data %>% select(z, variance_z, coping_Measure_tidy,`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit() 
n <- sum(n$`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`)
studies_effects <- Maladaptive_data %>% select( Study,
  z, variance_z, coping_Measure_tidy, `Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit()%>% group_by(Study) %>% summarise(n_effects = n())
n_studies <- nrow(studies_effects)

QM <- coping_measure_model_stats$cochran.qm
`QM p` <- coping_measure_model_stats$p.value.cochran.qm
b1 <- coping_measure_stats[2,] %>% select(Pearson_R)
z <- coping_measure_stats[2,] %>% select(estimate)
`estimate p` <- coping_measure_stats[2,] %>% select(p.value)
 coping_measure_data <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`) %>% mutate(QM_p = `QM p`,
                                                                              estimate_p= `estimate p`)
`Coping Measure` <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`)


```

```{r MAL_pairwise_coping_measure_comparison}
  pair_coping_measure <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~ 1 + coping_Measure_tidy_athlete_focus,
#  mods = ~ -1 + Region ,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 

pair_coping_measure_conf <- predict(pair_coping_measure)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
#coping_measure_M_perf <- model_performance(coping_measure)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

pair_coping_measure_stats <- broom::tidy(pair_coping_measure, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )

```

```{r MAL_coping_classification_mod}

#testing helmert coding, so that each level is contrasted against its next consecutive one
# Maladaptive_data$Coping_classification_helmert <- contrastable::set_contrasts(Maladaptive_data$Coping_classification ~ scaled_sum_code(n=3))
# Coping_classification_effect <- as.factor(Maladaptive_data$Coping_classification)
# contrasts(Maladaptive_data$Coping_classification_effect)<- contr.sum(levels(Maladaptive_data$Coping_classification_effect))
Maladaptive_data$Coping_classification_emot_ref <- relevel(
  Maladaptive_data$Coping_classification,
  ref = "Emotion Focused Coping"  
)
  
coping_classification <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~ -1+ Coping_classification_emot_ref, # not changed write up yet to accomodate change
# mods = ~ -1 + Var_1_summarised  ,
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + `Mean Age`,
#  mods = ~  + Coping_classification  * Risk_of_Bias ,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 

coping_classification_conf <- predict(coping_classification)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
coping_classification_M_perf <- model_performance(coping_classification)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

coping_classification_stats <- broom::tidy(coping_classification, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )
coping_classification_model_stats <- broom::glance(coping_classification)


# generating table stats
k <- coping_classification_model_stats$nobs
n <- Maladaptive_data %>% select(z, variance_z, coping_Measure_tidy,`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit() 
n <- sum(n$`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`)
studies_effects <- Maladaptive_data %>% select( Study,
  z, variance_z, Coping_classification, `Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit()%>% group_by(Study) %>% summarise(n_effects = n())
n_studies <- nrow(studies_effects)

QM <- coping_classification_model_stats$cochran.qm
`QM p` <- coping_classification_model_stats$p.value.cochran.qm
b1 <- coping_classification_stats[2,] %>% select(Pearson_R)
z <- coping_classification_stats[2,] %>% select(estimate)
`estimate p` <- coping_classification_stats[2,] %>% select(p.value)
 coping_classification_data <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`) %>% mutate(QM_p = `QM p`,                                                                           estimate_p= `estimate p`)
 
`Coping Classification` <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`)


```

```{r MAL_pairwise_coping_classification_comparison}
  pair_coping_measure <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~ 1 + Coping_classification_emot_ref,
#  mods = ~ -1 + Region ,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 

pair_Coping_classification_conf <- predict(pair_coping_measure)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
#coping_measure_M_perf <- model_performance(coping_measure)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

pair_Coping_classification_stats <- broom::tidy(pair_coping_measure, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )

```

```{r, MAL_mean_age_mod}
Mean_Age <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~ `Mean Age`,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 


Mean_Age_conf <- predict(Mean_Age)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
Mean_Age_M_perf <- model_performance(Mean_Age)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

Mean_Age_stats <- broom::tidy(Mean_Age, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )
Mean_Age_model_stats <- broom::glance(Mean_Age)


# generating table stats
k <- Mean_Age_model_stats$nobs
n <- Maladaptive_data %>% select(z, variance_z, `Mean Age`,`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit() 
n <- sum(n$`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`)

studies_effects <- Maladaptive_data %>% select( Study,
  z, variance_z, `Mean Age`, `Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit()%>% group_by(Study) %>% summarise(n_effects = n())
n_studies <- nrow(studies_effects)
QM <- Mean_Age_model_stats$cochran.qm
`QM p` <- Mean_Age_model_stats$p.value.cochran.qm
b1 <- Mean_Age_stats[2,] %>% select(Pearson_R)
z <- Mean_Age_stats[2,] %>% select(estimate)
`estimate p` <- Mean_Age_stats[2,] %>% select(p.value)
 Mean_Age_data <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`) %>% mutate(QM_p = `QM p`,
                                                                              estimate_p= `estimate p`)
`Mean Age` <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`)

```

```{r Mal_alpha_mod}
alpha <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~`Var 2 Alpha`,
#  dfs="contain",
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + Coping_classification  ,
# mods = ~ -1 + Var_1_summarised  ,
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + `Mean Age`,
#  mods = ~  + Coping_classification  * Risk_of_Bias ,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 

alpha_conf <- predict(alpha)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
alpha_M_perf <- model_performance(alpha)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

alpha_stats <- broom::tidy( alpha, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )
alpha_model_stats <- broom::glance( alpha)


# generating table stats
k <- alpha_model_stats$nobs
n <- Maladaptive_data %>% select(z, variance_z, `Var 2 Alpha`,`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit() 
n <- sum(n$`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`)

studies_effects <- Maladaptive_data %>% select( Study,
  z, variance_z, `Var 2 Alpha`, `Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit()%>% group_by(Study) %>% summarise(n_effects = n())
n_studies <- nrow(studies_effects)

QM <- alpha_model_stats$cochran.qm
`QM p` <- alpha_model_stats$p.value.cochran.qm
b1 <- alpha_stats[2,] %>% select(Pearson_R)
z <- alpha_stats[2,] %>% select(estimate)
`estimate p` <- alpha_stats[2,] %>% select(p.value)
 alpha_data <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`) %>% mutate(QM_p = `QM p`,
                                                                              estimate_p= `estimate p`)
`Alpha data` <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`)


```

```{r MAL_alpha_NA_mod}
alpha_NA <- rma.mv(
  yi = z,
  V = variance_z,
  mods = ~ +1 +`Var 2 Alpha_NA`,
#  dfs="contain",
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + Coping_classification  ,
# mods = ~ -1 + Var_1_summarised  ,
#  mods = ~ -1 + Risk_of_Bias + `Mean Age`,
#  mods = ~  + Coping_classification  * Risk_of_Bias ,
  data = Maladaptive_data,
  random = ~ 1 | Author_Year/effect_id,
  test = "t",
  method = "REML"
) 

alpha_NA_conf <- predict(alpha_NA)
# conf$ci.lb
# conf$ci.ub
alpha_NA_M_perf <- model_performance(alpha_NA)
# Compute robust CHE results for rho
# CHEresultrobust <- robust(m.gen, cluster = Study_id, clubSandwich = TRUE, digits = 3)
# CHEresultrobust

alpha_NA_stats <- broom::tidy( alpha_NA, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
  mutate(
         Pearson_R = convert_z2r(estimate),
         Pearson_low = convert_z2r(conf.low),
         Pearson_high = convert_z2r(conf.high)
         )
alpha_NA_model_stats <- broom::glance( alpha_NA)


# generating table stats
k <- alpha_NA_model_stats$nobs
n <- Maladaptive_data %>% select(z, variance_z, `Var 2 Alpha_NA`,`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit() 
n <- sum(n$`Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`)

studies_effects <- Maladaptive_data %>% select( Study,
  z, variance_z, `Var 2 Alpha_NA`, `Sample Size of Correlation Analysis`) %>% na.omit()%>% group_by(Study) %>% summarise(n_effects = n())
n_studies <- nrow(studies_effects)

QM <- alpha_NA_model_stats$cochran.qm
`QM p` <- alpha_NA_model_stats$p.value.cochran.qm
b1 <- alpha_NA_stats[2,] %>% select(Pearson_R)
z <- alpha_NA_stats[2,] %>% select(estimate)
`estimate p` <- alpha_NA_stats[2,] %>% select(p.value)
 alpha_NA_data <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`) %>% mutate(QM_p = `QM p`,
                                                                              estimate_p= `estimate p`)
`Alpha NA data` <- cbind(n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`, b1, z, `estimate p`)


```

```{r}
# anova(
#   m.gen1, 
# #  m.gen2, 
#    m.gen3,
#  #  m.gen4,
# #  m.gen5,
#   refit = TRUE
# )
```

The study characteristics of percentage of female participants, mean participant age, coping classification and coping measure were examined in moderator analyses to asses their potential impact on the association between maladaptive coping strategies and anxiety severity.

Female athletes are identified has having significantly higher levels of anxiety in comparison to their male athletes (source), and so the percentage of female participants was investigated as a potential moderator. For the maladaptive strategies, all studies reported data on gender. Moderator analysis showed that the percentage of female participants had no significant effect on the association between adaptive coping and anxiety (k = `r Percentage_Female$k`, n = `r Percentage_Female$n`, $QM$ = `r round(Percentage_Female$QM,2)`, $p$ = `r round(Percentage_Female$QM_p,3)`). The pooled moderating effect size of Percentage of female participants was negligible and not significant ($r$ = `r round(Percentage_Female$Pearson_R,2)`, 95% CI\[`r round(gender_raw_stats[2,10],3)`, `r round(gender_raw_stats[2,11],3)`\]).

As previous meta analyses have identified mean age of participants to be a significant moderator between coping and mental health outcomes in non-athletic populations (source), it was explored as a potential moderator in elite athletes. Moderator analysis demonstrated mean age of participants was not a significant moderator (k = `r Mean_Age_data$k`, n = `r Mean_Age_data$n`, $QM$ = `r round(Mean_Age_data$QM,3)`, $p$ = `r round(Mean_Age_data$QM_p,3)`). The pooled moderating effect size of mean participant age was negligible and not significant ($r$ = `r round(Mean_Age_stats[2,9], 3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(Mean_Age_stats[2,10], 3)`,`r round(Mean_Age_stats[2,11], 3)`\]).

Coping classification moderated the association between coping and anxiety levels in elite athletes (k = `r coping_classification_data$k`, n = `r coping_classification_data$n`, $QM$ = `r round(coping_classification_data$QM,2)`, $p$ \< .`r round(coping_classification_data$QM_p,3)`01). All classifications of maladaptive coping significantly associated with anxiety. Problem focused coping had the strongest pooled effect ($r$ = `r round(coping_classification_stats[4,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(coping_classification_stats[4,10],3)`, `r round(coping_classification_stats[4,11],3)`\]), followed by combined coping ($r$ = `r round(coping_classification_stats[3,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(coping_classification_stats[3,10],3)`, `r round(coping_classification_stats[3,11],3)`\]), emotion focused coping ($r$ = `r round(coping_classification_stats[1,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(coping_classification_stats[1,10],3)`, `r round(coping_classification_stats[1,11],3)`\]), and avoidance focused coping ($r$ = `r round(coping_classification_stats[2,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(coping_classification_stats[2,10],3)`, `r round(coping_classification_stats[2,11],3)`\]) all had significant positive pooled effect sizes. Posthoc pairwise comparisons found no significant differences between maladaptive emotion focused coping and other forms of maladaptive coping ($p$ \> .05). Despite emotion focused coping having a significant association with anxiety, the results failed to reject the null hypothesis for maladaptive emotion focused coping having the strongest association with anxiety in elite athletes. This suggests that increased use of any maladaptive strategy attributed to these domains should be recognised as anxiety risk factors in elite athletes.

Due to the limited number of studies using athlete specific maladaptive measures of coping (n = 2), the decision was made to cluster the COPE/MCOPE/TOPS hybrid used by Hets... & Chroni, (????) with the TOPS in the moderator analysis on the effect of coping measure on the association between maladaptive coping and anxiety. The influence of coping measures significantly moderated the association between maladaptive coping and anxiety levels in elite athletes (k = `r coping_measure_data$k`, n = `r coping_measure_data$n`, $QM$ = `r round(coping_measure_data$QM,2)`, $p$ \< .`r round(coping_measure_data$QM_p,3)`01).Studies using the Difficulties in Emotion Regulation scale reported the strongest significant positive pooled effect size ($r$ = `r round(coping_measure_stats[3,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(coping_measure_stats[3,10],3)`, `r round(coping_measure_stats[3,11],3)`\]). This was followed by studies using Brief Cope ($r$ = `r round(coping_measure_stats[2,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(coping_measure_stats[2,10],3)`, `r round(coping_measure_stats[2,11],3)`\]) and studies using the TOPS or COPE/COPE/TOPS hybrid ($r$ = `r round(coping_measure_stats[1,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(coping_measure_stats[1,10],3)`, `r round(coping_measure_stats[1,11],3)`\]). Studies evaluating maladaptive coping using the CISS did record a significant association with anxiety ($r$ = `r round(coping_measure_stats[4,9],3)`, 95% CI\[`r round(coping_measure_stats[4,10],3)`, `r round(coping_measure_stats[4,11],3)`\]). Post-hoc pairwise comparison meanwhile found no significant differences between the athlete specific measures and other measures ($p$ \> .05). This suggests studies using the Brief coping scale, the Difficulties in Emotion Regulation Scale, and athlete specific measures are more likely to demonstrate the use of their respective coping strategies as an anxiety risk factor, with little difference between them. The remaining measures had no significant pooled effect on the association between coping and anxiety, and their full details can be found in table ... .

Measurement reliability evaluated by Cronbach's Alpha was investigated as a moderator. Measurment reliability had a significant effect on the association between Maladaptive coping and anxiety (k = `r alpha_data$k`, n = `r alpha_data$n`, $QM$ = `r round(alpha_data$QM,2)`, $p$ \< .`r round(alpha_data$QM_p,3)`)01. The pooled moderating effect size of measurement reliability had a large positive significant effect ($r$ = `r round(alpha_stats$Pearson_R[2],2)`, 95% CI\[`r round(alpha_stats[2,10],3)`, `r round(alpha_stats[2,11],3)`\]). This suggests that studies using more reliable measures were more likely to record stronger positive associations between maladaptive coping strategies and anxiety severity. In the maladaptive strategies,only 7 effects associated within 1 study had not reported Cronbach's Alpha for their sample. Due to the limited number of missing values, the decision was made not to report the sensitivity analysis of the missing Cronbach's Alpha data of the maladaptive strategies.

```{r}
grand_moderator_table <- rbind(`Percentage Female`, #`Gender data`, 
                               `Mean Age` ,`Coping Classification`, `Coping Measure`, `Alpha data`)

grand_moderator_table %>% mutate(label_ID = row_number(),
                                 label = case_when(
                                   label_ID == "1"  ~ "Percentage Female",
                                #   label_ID == "2"  ~"Gender data present/missing", 
                                   label_ID == "2"  ~"Mean Age",
                                   label_ID == "3"  ~"Coping Classification",
                                   label_ID == "4"  ~"Coping Measure",
                                   label_ID == "5"  ~ "Coping Measure Reliability"
                                 )
                                 ) %>%
  select(label, n_studies, k, n, QM, `QM p`) %>%
  gt() %>% fmt_number(decimals = 4) %>% 
  tab_header(
    title = "Moderator fit Analysis"
  )

count_data_coping_classification <- Maladaptive_data %>%
  group_by(Coping_classification) %>%
  summarise(
    k = n(),
    n = sum(n)
            ) %>% as.data.frame() 

coping_classification_stats %>% as.data.frame() %>% select(-type) %>% 
  arrange(desc(estimate)) %>%
  mutate(term = str_remove(term,"Coping_classification"),
     #    n = count_data_coping_classification$n
         ) %>%
  left_join(
    y = count_data_coping_classification,
    by = join_by(term == Coping_classification),
  ) %>%
  select(term, k, n, estimate, Pearson_R, Pearson_low, Pearson_high, p.value) %>%
  gt() %>% fmt_number(decimals = 4)%>% 
  tab_header(
    title = "Coping Classification Moderator Analysis"
  )

count_data_coping_measure <- Maladaptive_data %>%
  group_by(coping_Measure_tidy) %>%
  summarise(
    k = n(),
    n = sum(n)
            ) %>% as.data.frame() 

coping_measure_stats %>% select(-type) %>% arrange(desc(estimate)) %>%
  mutate(term = str_remove(term,"coping_Measure_tidy")) %>%
  left_join(
    y = count_data_coping_measure,
    by = join_by(term == coping_Measure_tidy),
  ) %>%
  select(term, k, n, estimate, Pearson_R, Pearson_low, Pearson_high, p.value) %>%
  gt() %>% fmt_number(decimals = 4) %>% 
  tab_header(
    title = "Coping Measures Moderator Analysis"
  )
```

### 3.5. Synthesis of results

### 3.6. Risk of bias across studies

### 3.7. Additional analysis
